title: Fix dashboard data loading, actor availability, habits saving, pricing buttons, and add admin system

requirements.summary: Fix the 'actor not available' error on the dashboard and throughout the app by gating all React Query hooks on actor availability and displaying loading states
acceptanceCriteria: No 'actor not available' error appears on the dashboard after login
All React Query hooks in useQueries.ts gate execution on actor availability (enabled flag)
Loading spinners or skeletons are shown while the actor is initializing
Activity logging on the dashboard works successfully after login

file_operations.description: Add actor availability gating to all React Query hooks by checking `!!actor && !isFetching` in the enabled flag for every query. Add loading state handling that properly reflects actor initialization status in custom return objects, similar to the authorization component's useGetCallerUserProfile pattern. Ensure mutations do not execute until actor is ready by adding defensive checks.

Add loading spinners or skeleton components that display while the actor is initializing. Check actorFetching state before rendering data-dependent components. Ensure the AddActivityForm and ActivityTimeline components are not rendered until actor is available.

Add actor availability check before allowing form submission. Display a disabled state or loading indicator if actor is not ready. Show appropriate error messaging if activity logging fails due to actor unavailability.
Add loading state handling that waits for actor availability before fetching activities. Display a skeleton loader or spinner while actor is initializing or activities are loading.

requirements.summary: Fix dashboard data not loading by ensuring all dashboard data (stats, today's activities, habit progress bars) is fetched and rendered correctly after the actor becomes available
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard stats (hours, earnings, productivity) load and display correctly
Today's activity timeline renders fetched activities
Habit progress bars display current progress from fetched data
Data refreshes correctly when the user logs a new activity or changes the date filter

file_operations.description: Review and fix query keys for activities and habits queries to ensure proper caching and invalidation. Ensure useGetActivities includes proper dependency on userId, habitId filter, and isProductive filter. Add query invalidation for 'activities' and 'habits' keys after successful logActivity mutation. Verify that all queries have proper refetch and staleTime configurations.

Fix data fetching logic to properly derive stats from activities query results. Ensure selectedDate state changes trigger refetch of activities. Add error boundaries and fallback UI for failed queries. Verify that habit progress calculations use the correct filtered activities data.
Ensure the component properly handles loading and error states. Add null/undefined checks for stat values before rendering.
Fix the progress calculation logic to correctly filter activities by habit, date range, and productivity. Ensure the component updates when activities data changes. Add loading and error states.

requirements.summary: Fix habit creation on the HabitsPage so that new habits are saved to the backend and appear immediately in the habits list
acceptanceCriteria: A new habit submitted via HabitFormModal is saved to the backend canister
The habits list updates immediately after a successful save
An error toast is shown if saving fails
The habit form modal closes after a successful save

file_operations.description: Review useCreateHabit mutation to ensure it correctly calls actor.createHabit with all required parameters (name, goalType, goalValue, color). Add proper error handling and ensure onSuccess callback invalidates the 'habits' query key to trigger refetch. Verify the mutation is not attempting to execute before actor is available.

Fix form submission handler to properly call the createHabit mutation with validated form data. Ensure the modal closes only after successful mutation (in onSuccess callback). Add loading state during mutation execution. Display error toast on mutation failure. Verify all required fields (name, goalType, goalValue, color) are included in the mutation call.

Ensure the habits list properly reacts to query invalidation after habit creation. Add loading state while habits are being fetched. Handle empty state when no habits exist. Verify the habit creation flow triggers query refetch.

requirements.summary: Wire up all buttons on the PricingPage so plan selection navigates authenticated users to PaymentPage and prompts unauthenticated users to log in
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking a plan button on PricingPage navigates to PaymentPage with the correct plan selected
Unauthenticated users are redirected to LoginPage when clicking a plan button
No console errors occur when any pricing page button is clicked
All CTA buttons on the pricing page respond to click events

file_operations.description: Wire up all plan selection buttons to check authentication state using useInternetIdentity hook. If authenticated, navigate to /payment with plan parameter using router navigation. If not authenticated, redirect to /login using router navigation. Remove any placeholder or non-functional button handlers. Ensure all CTA buttons have working onClick handlers. Add URL parameter handling to pass selected plan to the payment page.

requirements.summary: Add frontend admin access control by exposing an isAdmin query, showing admin navigation links only for admins, and protecting the AdminPage route with principal-based checks
acceptanceCriteria: Admin navigation links in the Sidebar are visible only to the admin user
Non-admin users who navigate to /admin are redirected to /dashboard
AdminPage loads correctly for the admin user without the password gate blocking them
isAdmin state is derived from the backend principal check, not hardcoded on the frontend

file_operations.description: Add a new useIsAdmin query hook that calls actor.isCallerAdmin() to check if the current user is an admin. Gate the query on actor availability using the enabled flag pattern (!!actor && !isFetching). Return proper loading state that reflects actor initialization. Use the authorization component's pattern for consistent state handling.

Import and use the useIsAdmin hook to conditionally render the admin navigation link. Only show the admin link when isAdmin returns true. Add loading state handling to avoid flickering during admin check. Ensure the admin link is hidden for non-admin users.

Add principal-based admin protection using the useIsAdmin hook. If isAdmin is false and not loading, redirect to /dashboard using router navigation. Remove or supplement the existing session-storage password gate with the principal-based check, giving priority to the backend admin check. Show a loading spinner while the admin check is in progress. Only render the admin panel content after confirming the user is an admin.

Add route-level admin protection for the /admin route by wrapping it in a guard that checks isAdmin status. Redirect non-admin users attempting to access /admin to /dashboard. Ensure the route guard respects loading states and does not redirect prematurely.
